achievements.title: Early Korean genome-wide research on antidepressant response
achievements.summary: Research on genetic variants associated with antidepressant treatment response in Korean patients, including a genome-wide association study and related genetic prediction work.
achievements.evidence: See selected publications and the linked Google Scholar profile.

achievements.title: Contributions to East Asian and cross-ancestry psychiatric genomics
achievements.summary: Research on shared and ancestry-specific genetic architecture involving psychiatric disorders, subjective well-being, educational attainment, occupational attainment, and related phenotypes.

achievements.title: Clinical trials of neuromodulation and digital therapeutics
achievements.summary: Clinical research includes home-based transcranial direct current stimulation for depressive and bipolar depressive symptoms, digital therapeutics, and related neurophysiological assessment.

achievements.title: Psychiatric measurement and network-analysis research
achievements.summary: Development and validation of Korean mood-instability and affective-temperament measures, and network analyses of depressive, anxiety, borderline-personality, ADHD, and interpersonal-sensitivity symptoms.

achievements.title: Population-based research on environmental and neuropsychiatric risk
achievements.summary: Studies using national and population datasets to examine suicide, psychological distress, dementia, psychosis, panic attacks, sleep, temperature, air pollution, and other environmental exposures.

achievements.title: Leadership of a hospital-based precision-medicine program
achievements.summary: As Director of the Center for Precision Medicine at Seoul National University Bundang Hospital, the public CV describes efforts to establish an EMR-based biobank cohort and advance genomic research.

achievements.title: International consortium collaboration
achievements.summary: Research contributions include work with the Psychiatric Genomics Consortium and the Global Burden of Disease consortium.

achievements.title: Principal-investigator and sub-principal-investigator research support
achievements.summary: The January 24, 2026 CV lists funded projects from the National Research Foundation of Korea, KHIDI/Ministry of Health and Welfare, NIPA, and the U.S. National Institute of Mental Health, covering neuropsychiatric disorders, dementia and psychiatric genetics, neuromodulation, and digital therapeutics.

interpretation_note: The entries are a concise, public summary. They do not replace grant award documents, institutional announcements, or original publications.
